query: What is the current state of polling and political dynamics for Zambia's August 2026 presidential election, specifically Hakainde Hichilema's expected vote share? Include recent 2025-2026 polls, opposition coalition strength, whether Edgar Lungu/PF is running (note Lungu died in 2025), and turnout factors.
answer:
I've completed comprehensive research on Zambia's August 2026 presidential election. Here's the summary of key findings:

**Election:** 13 August 2026; incumbent **Hakainde Hichilema (UPND)** seeks a second term (won 2021 with ~59%). 14 candidates, 8.8M registered voters.

**Hichilema's expected vote share:** The best data — the academic **ZERN survey** (fieldwork June–Nov 2025) — shows **55% voting intention** for Hichilema, 13% opposition, and a large **32% undecided**. Informal online polls corroborate low-to-mid 50s (~52–55%). Reuters (July 2026) calls him "favoured to win" but facing a "strong challenge." **Caveat:** The 55% figure predates opposition consolidation, so likely overstates his current lead. No rigorous probability-sample horserace poll exists.

**Opposition coalition:** Consolidated around the **Tonse–Pamodzi Alliance**, with **Brian Mundubile** elected candidate (28 Jan 2026), running under the **NRPUP** banner with Makebi Zulu. Strong rallies and momentum, but the broader opposition remains **fragmented** (M'membe's People's Pact, Kalaba's Orange Alliance) — which analysts say benefits UPND.

**Lungu/PF:** **Edgar Lungu died 5 June 2025** (not a candidate); prolonged burial dispute followed. **PF is deeply divided** — Sampa faction joined UPND, Lubinda faction backs Mundubile's ticket — and cannot run under its own name. Mundubile campaigns on Lungu's legacy.

**Turnout:** One analyst projects >82% (2021 was ~70%). Analysts flag **voter apathy** among 2021 UPND supporters (cost-of-living frustration) as the biggest risk to Hichilema, despite macroeconomic recovery (GDP 4.3%, inflation 6.5%, reserves $6.5bn).

Full structured findings with all sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: 2026 Zambian general election - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: General elections are due to be held in Zambia on 13 August 2026 to elect a president, members of the national assembly, councillors and council chairs.

sources.title: Main findings from the Zambia Election Research Network Round 1 Survey
sources.snippet: 55% planned to vote for Hichilema/UPND; 13% opposition; 32% didn't know/refused; 51% expected free and fair elections.

sources.title: Zambia election will put Hichilema's economic record to the test (Reuters)
sources.snippet: Hichilema favoured to win a second term but may face a strong challenge from opposition leader Brian Mundubile; GDP 4.3%, inflation 6.5%, reserves $6.5bn.

sources.title: Zambia: Alliance politics take centre stage as Zambia heads to August polls
sources.snippet: 14 presidential contenders, 8.8 million registered voters, 13,529 polling stations; UPND Alliance vs Tonse-Pamodzi, People's Pact, Orange Alliance; PF deeply divided.

sources.title: Mundubile elected Tonse Alliance presidential candidate
sources.snippet: Brian Mundubile elected Tonse Alliance chairperson and presidential candidate on 28 January 2026, defeating Danny Pule 129-34.

sources.title: Candidate Profiles: Brian Mundubile - Open Zambia
sources.snippet: Mundubile is the Tonse-PF Pamodzi Alliance candidate, running with Makebi Zulu on an NRPUP ticket; campaign centers on honoring Edgar Lungu's legacy.

sources.title: Death and state funeral of Edgar Lungu - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Edgar Lungu died 5 June 2025 in Pretoria, South Africa, age 68; prolonged burial dispute; Pretoria High Court ruled 8 August 2025 for repatriation.

sources.title: HH wins 2026 presidential opinion poll among top six candidates
sources.snippet: Zambian Post Facebook poll of ~67,600 participants projected Hichilema at 55%.

sources.title: Hichilema dominates Mwamba online poll with clear 52% majority
sources.snippet: Emmanuel Mwamba Facebook poll (March 2026) showed Hichilema ~52% among four candidates.

sources.title: 2026 Election Poll Survey Analysis - Zambia Streets
sources.snippet: Provincial projections: UPND strong in Southern 74%, Western 68%, NW 66%; NRPUP/Mundubile leads Luapula 76%, Eastern, Northern, Muchinga. No methodology disclosed.

sources.title: 14 presidential candidates validly nominated for 2026 poll
sources.snippet: ECZ validly nominated 14 presidential candidates including Hichilema (UPND) with running mate Mutale Nalumango.

sources.title: Zambia scenario analysis predict over 82% voter turnout on 13th August
sources.snippet: Single-analyst (Prof. Miyanda) projection of >82% turnout on 13 August 2026.

sources.title: The biggest opposition to Hichilema may be voter apathy
sources.snippet: Analysis argues voter apathy, not opposition parties, is the greatest challenge to UPND.

sources.title: Elections 2026: is Zambia's democratic success story beginning to fray?
sources.snippet: ISS analysis warns of legal manoeuvring and democratic backsliding concerns around the August election.

data_points.metric: Opposition party voting intention (ZERN Round 1 survey)
Believed 2026 elections will be completely free and fair (ZERN)
Hichilema support (Zambian Post Facebook poll, ~67,600 participants)
Mundubile votes in Tonse-Pamodzi Alliance leadership election
UPND projected vote share Southern Province (unsourced provincial projection)
Age of Edgar Lungu at death
